title: lupinus lepidus subsp. lyallii
intro: Lupinus lepidus subsp. lyallii is a subspecies of the lupine plant, also known as a wild lupine or Lyall's lupine, which is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of Lupinus lepidus subsp. lyallii include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using a tincture to extract its medicinal compounds.
